pr.description: List PRs for a repo (/pr or /pr <repo-name>)
pr.template: If $ARGUMENTS is provided, use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?name=<url-encoded-name>` with the given name to resolve the repo ID. Otherwise, detect from the current directory: use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?dir=<pwd-url-encoded>` with the current directory path to resolve the repo ID. If the API returns null, say "Repo not found." Otherwise, use the bughunter prlist tool with the resolved repo to list all PRs.

prs.description: List pull requests for this repo
prs.template: First, detect whether this project is registered as a GrepLoop repository: use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?dir=<pwd-url-encoded>` with the current directory path to resolve the repo ID. If it returns null, say "This project is not registered in GrepLoop." Otherwise, use the bughunter prlist tool to list all PRs for the current repo.

prcheck.template: First, detect whether this project is registered as a GrepLoop repository: use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?dir=<pwd-url-encoded>` with the current directory path to resolve the repo ID. If it returns null, say "This project is not registered in GrepLoop." Otherwise, use the bughunter prcheck tool to review PR #$ARGUMENTS. Show me the rating and any findings.

prcheckstatus.template: First, detect whether this project is registered as a GrepLoop repository: use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?dir=<pwd-url-encoded>` with the current directory path to resolve the repo ID. If it returns null, say "This project is not registered in GrepLoop." Otherwise, use the bughunter prcheckstatus tool with branch=$ARGUMENTS to check the status.

bugfixer.description: Auto-fix loop: review → fix → re-review until 4/5 (/bugfixer <number>)
bugfixer.template: First, detect whether this project is registered as a GrepLoop repository: use a web fetch to call `http://localhost:3300/api/repos/resolve?dir=<pwd-url-encoded>` with the current directory path to resolve the repo ID. If it returns null, say "This project is not registered in GrepLoop." Otherwise, use the bughunter prcheck tool to review PR #$1. If rating >= 4/5, report PASS. Otherwise fix each finding (read the file, apply the fix, commit with 'fix: address review findings'), then tell me to run /bugfixer $1 again for re-review. Loop until 4/5.
